title: Glasgow Airport and New York united once again with return of direct flights

sections.heading: What happened
sections.content: United Airlines has confirmed it is to return to Glasgow Airport in 2026 to reinstate direct flights to its New York hub, Newark Liberty International Airport.

sections.heading: Key details
sections.content: Glasgow Airport and New York united once again with return of direct flights The new daily scheduled service will take off on 8 May and run until 23 September 2026. It is the first time United will have operated at Glasgow since 2019.

sections.heading: Why it matters
sections.content: The Glasgow-New York/Newark flight will be operated by Boeing 737 MAX-8 aircraft with a total of 166 seats, 16 of which are Premium Economy seats, 54 Economy Plus seats and 96 Economy seats. United Airlines’ hub at Newark Liberty International Airport (EWR) is its largest East Coast hub and a significant base for both domestic and international flights, especially to Europe. The hub connects passengers to destinations across North America, Latin America, the Caribbean, Europe, and the Middle East. The route has already been hailed as a "landmark achievement" which will bring "considerable cultural and tourism benefits" by business and civic leaders.
